title: Monetary Survey (Summary of Monetary Accounts of Bank Markazi Iran, Commercial Banks and Specialized Banks), Iran, 1963/64-1969/70 (World Bank, 1971)
source_org: International Bank for Reconstruction and Development (World Bank), data sourced by the Bank from Bank Markazi Iran
extraction_method: MANUAL TRANSCRIPTION, not bulk download. Source document is data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/1971_cep_vol7_statistical_annex.pdf (Iran - Current economic position and prospects, Vol. 7 of 7: Statistical annex, May 18 1971), a scanned typewriter-era World Bank report. Table 6.1 is on PDF page 69. Rendered at 300 DPI via `pdftoppm -r 300 -f 69 -l 70 -png` and read directly from the rendered PNG using the Read tool (this page's print quality was substantially better than Table 5.2's page, so no further crop/zoom passes were needed). Every row and total was cross-checked by internal balance-sheet arithmetic (this table is a monetary-survey balance sheet, so assets must equal liabilities and subtotals must equal their components) -- ALL SEVEN YEAR-COLUMNS reconciled EXACTLY on every check: (a) 'Public sector (net)' = Claims on public sector minus Deposits of public sector, for all 7 years; (b) 'Assets = Liabilities' = Foreign assets (net) + Public sector (net) + Claims on private sector, for all 7 years; (c) 'Money supply' = Currency + Demand deposits, for all 7 years; (d) 'Assets = Liabilities' = Money supply + Time and savings deposits + Importers' deposits + Capital accounts + Other items (net) + SDR allocation, for all 7 years. This exhaustive cross-checking caught and corrected two initial misreadings: (1) 'Claims on public sector' for 1968/69 was initially misread as '83.1' but the Public-sector-net identity (88.1-40.1=48.0, matching the printed 48.0) proved it must be '88.1' (a 3/8 typewriter-glyph confusion); (2) 'Capital accounts' for 1965/66 was initially misread as '28.5' but the Assets=Liabilities balance-sheet identity for that column only closed exactly (142.7) if this cell were '28.9' (a 5/9 confusion) -- both corrections are reflected in the final data. Two minor sub-line splits are preserved as printed rather than force-split: for 1968/69 and 1969/70, the source itself groups the 'Treasury' and 'Plan Organization' claims/deposits sub-lines together in a single bracket total (rather than printing them as two separate numbers, as it does for all earlier years) -- transcribed as a combined 'Treasury and Plan Organization (combined)' row for exactly those two columns, with the individual Treasury/Plan Organization cells left blank for 1968/69-1969/70 to avoid fabricating a split the source doesn't provide. No value was interpolated, estimated, or fabricated. Dashes ('--' or '-') in the source for Importers' deposits (1963/64) and SDR allocation (1963/64-1968/69) are recorded as 0, consistent with the balance-sheet identity checks above which require them to be exactly zero contributions.
units: billion rials (1 US$ = 75.7 rials per the companion report's stated exchange rate)
license_terms: World Bank Archives — public disclosure authorized document
notes: Source: Table 6.1, page 69 of the Statistical Annex, source line 'Bank Markazi Iran' (the Central Bank of Iran, founded 1960). Footnote 1/: summary of monetary accounts of Bank Markazi Iran, commercial banks, and specialized banks (i.e. a consolidated banking-system balance sheet, the standard IMF/IFS-style 'monetary survey' format). Footnote 2/: the Currency/Demand-deposits split and the Time-and-savings-deposits figures are based on END-OF-YEAR position and will differ from Table 6.3 (Credit Expansion by Commercial and Specialized Banks, not yet extracted) which uses MONTHLY AVERAGES instead. This table directly complements the already-extracted wb1960-changes-in-money-supply-1957-1959 and wb1960-banking-statistics-private-sector-1957-1959 datasets in this same directory, extending Iran's money-supply/banking time series forward from 1959 to 1970 with only a 3-year gap (1960/61-1962/63 not covered by this table). Money supply nearly doubled 1963/64-1969/70 (48.9 to 90.5 billion rials) while Time and savings deposits nearly quadrupled (32.5 to 117.1 billion rials), and Claims on private sector (bank credit to the private sector) grew 2.7x (72.9 to 197.9 billion rials) -- consistent with the rapid financial deepening of the Iranian economy during the Fourth Development Plan period. Part of a systematic effort to extract Pahlavi-era budget/money-supply/banking data from primary-source World Bank PDFs in data/raw/world-bank-archives-iran/historical-documents/, following the same visual-verification methodology as the wb1960-* and wb1971-ordinary-budget-*/wb1971-government-revenue-* datasets in this same directory.
